nodes.label: Production Image Digest Pinning
nodes.rationale: Artifact Registry tags are mutable — a tag can be repointed after the fact (accidentally or via a compromised credential), letting an uninspected image slip into a promote-this-validated-build deploy. resolve-image therefore resolves immutable content digests for both api and migrate images and production pins API_IMAGE/MIGRATE_IMAGE by @sha256 digest, never mutable tag. Production never rebuilds; it promotes the exact integration build.

nodes.label: GCE-VM-per-Environment Architecture (supersedes Cloud Run)
nodes.source_location: Why GCE instead of Cloud Run (superseding RIZ-36)
nodes.rationale: RIZ-36's Cloud Run design left an explicit gap: the TimescaleDB extension required by the continuous-aggregate migrations (daily_app_totals, daily_category_totals, hourly_category_totals) is unsupported by Cloud SQL, Neon, and Supabase. Running the entire stack — including TimescaleDB itself, the same image as local dev — as one docker compose unit on a small GCE VM per environment makes the extension present by construction. Trade-off accepted deliberately: the project now owns VM patching/uptime/backups instead of a managed provider, in exchange for dropping the extension-support gap.

nodes.label: Expand/Contract Migration Backward-Compatibility Invariant
nodes.rationale: Because migrations commit before the api container is (re)started, there is always a window where the already-migrated schema is served by the still-running previous api revision. Therefore every migration must be backward-compatible with the previous application revision: expand steps are additive only; destructive contract steps ship as a separate later migration, only after a revision that no longer uses the old shape is deployed and stable.

edges.source_location: Why GCE instead of Cloud Run (superseding RIZ-36)
